title: BootRecord
description: Snapshot written by bin/bootstrap.sh and bin/retrofit.sh before they mutate a target repo. Records every reversible action plus a copy of the pre-state bytes for each touched file (in a sibling `pre-state/` directory). Consumed by bin/undo-bootstrap.sh to reverse a run. Lives at memory/.nyann/bootstraps/<ISO-ts>/manifest.json — committed by default so a teammate who pulls the bootstrap PR can also undo it.

schema_version.description: BootRecord schema version. Bump in lockstep with the schema's $id when v2 ships.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when bootstrap/retrofit started writing the record.

target.description: Absolute path of the target repo at record time.

source.description: Which orchestrator produced the record. undo-bootstrap reverses both uniformly; the field is informational + lets future tooling filter.

profile_sha256.description: Hex SHA-256 of the canonicalised (jq -Sc) profile JSON in effect during the run. Informational — undo works off the manifest, not the live profile — but a mismatch surfaces in audit reports.

plan_sha256.description: Hex SHA-256 of the canonicalised ActionPlan that drove the run. Same value bootstrap.sh's --plan-sha256 binding checked.

actions.description: Ordered list of mutations performed. Order matters: undo walks this list in reverse so a `branch` created on top of a `seed-commit` is removed before the seed commit itself.

Category.description: Loose grouping for --scope filtering in undo-bootstrap. Mirrors DriftReport.scope_applied where possible (no `history` — history-affecting actions like seed-commit fall under `branching`).

path.description: Repo-relative path of the touched file.

pre_existed.description: True if the file (or any inode) was at `path` before the run.

pre_state_blob.description: Filename inside the sibling `pre-state/` directory holding the original bytes. Required when `pre_existed` is true; absent otherwise. Flat layout, basename only — no subdirectories.

pre_state_sha256.description: Hex SHA-256 of the pre_state_blob contents. Lets undo verify the blob hasn't been corrupted/swapped before restoring.

post_state_sha256.description: Hex SHA-256 of the file's bytes immediately after bootstrap mutated it. Set by br_finalize_writes when the file exists at finalize time. undo-bootstrap compares current bytes against this to detect post-bootstrap edits — a mismatch means the user touched the file and undo refuses without --force.

reversible.description: Whether undo can mechanically reverse this write. False for actions where the pre-state is non-recoverable (e.g., the file was a symlink whose target is now ambiguous).

irreversible_reason.description: Required when `reversible` is false; explains the obstruction in human-readable form.

SeedCommitAction.description: Empty seed commit created when bootstrap encountered an unborn HEAD.

BranchAction.description: Long-lived branch created (e.g., develop under gitflow). `base_sha` is the commit the branch pointed at when created — undo refuses if the branch has moved (new commits stacked).

DefaultBranchRenameAction.description: Default branch rename (typically `master` → `main`). Reversal flips the rename if HEAD still matches `to`.
